purpose: Fix and harden the existing Hermes worker adapter implementation after code review. This is not a new feature roadmap; it is a concrete correction and quality track for the current worker/core Hermes code.

review_basis.summary: The Hermes adapter has a solid phase-1 foundation, but it needs semantic fixes, policy consistency, real task-flow integration, endpoint hardening, cleaner audit events, and stricter tests before production-style use.

global_principles: Hermes remains an external_agent_worker only, never the control plane.
Fix correctness before adding new capabilities.
Prefer explicit denial over permissive fallback.
All successful Hermes results must become Ananta artifacts.
All cloud/context/security decisions must be deterministic and traceable.
Tests must cover both positive and negative paths for every fix.

milestones.title: Endpoint, cloud and context policy hardening
HTTP client and health check robustness

tasks.title: Fix plan_only validation mode
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter currently validates plan_only as summarize. This works only accidentally because both use the common schema today. It is semantically wrong and will break when plan-specific validation is added.
implementation_notes: Remove the special-case mapping mode if mode != 'plan_only' else 'summarize'.
Teach validate_payload_for_mode to accept mode='plan_only' explicitly.
Add plan_only-specific schema/validation if useful, but do not make it stricter than current behavior unless tests prove compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: HermesAdapter passes mode='plan_only' to validate_payload_for_mode for plan_only executions.
validate_payload_for_mode handles 'plan_only' explicitly and returns no error for valid common plan payloads.
A unit test fails before the fix and passes after: monkeypatch validate_payload_for_mode and assert it receives mode='plan_only'.
Existing plan_only success tests still pass.
No summarize behavior changes accidentally.
test_requirements: Add or update parser validation test for plan_only.
Add adapter test asserting correct validation mode.
anti_patterns: Do not keep hidden aliasing from plan_only to summarize.
Do not weaken common schema validation to hide the issue.

tasks.title: Unify review/code_review/verify capability naming
tasks.problem: Hermes profile allows code_review, ToolRouter uses review, and HermesAdapter accepts review/code_review/verify. This creates inconsistent policy behavior between CapabilityRegistry, ToolRouter and adapter runtime.
implementation_notes: Pick one canonical public capability for review. Recommended: keep both review and code_review as aliases during migration, but normalize internally.
CapabilityRegistry.hermes_capability_decision should allow review and code_review if adapter accepts both.
ToolRouter should not claim Hermes supports capabilities that CapabilityRegistry denies.
acceptance_criteria: HERMES_ALLOWED_CAPABILITIES includes both 'review' and 'code_review', or a documented normalization layer maps one to the other everywhere.
CapabilityRegistry.hermes_capability_decision('review') returns allowed=true.
CapabilityRegistry.hermes_capability_decision('code_review') returns allowed=true.
ToolRoutingService route requiring review can select Hermes when enabled and available.
HermesAdapter.review accepts an envelope with review capability and an envelope with code_review capability.
verify is either explicitly documented as an accepted review alias or removed from HermesAdapter.review acceptance.
test_requirements: Add tests for review, code_review, and verify behavior.
Add one test comparing ToolRouter and CapabilityRegistry decisions for review capability.
anti_patterns: Do not let router say Hermes can do review while CapabilityRegistry denies review.
Do not add new aliases without tests.

tasks.title: Normalize capability vocabulary between shell_execute and shell_execution
tasks.problem: ExecutionEnvelope uses shell_execute while ToolRoutingService uses shell_execution. Hermes denied capabilities include shell_execute, but ToolRouter checks shell_execution. Mixed vocabulary can cause missed denies or confusing routing.
implementation_notes: Introduce a small capability normalization helper used by ToolRouter and Hermes capability checks.
Do not silently allow unknown capability names.
Prefer canonical execution vocabulary from ExecutionEnvelope, but support legacy aliases for routing input.
acceptance_criteria: shell_execution and shell_execute normalize to one canonical denied capability for Hermes.
A request requiring shell_execution cannot route to Hermes.
A request requiring shell_execute cannot route to Hermes.
Denial reason includes the original requested name and canonical denied capability where practical.
Existing command_execute tests still pass.
test_requirements: Add two ToolRouter tests: required_capabilities=['shell_execution'] and ['shell_execute'] both deny Hermes.
Add CapabilityRegistry test for alias handling or explicit denial.
anti_patterns: Do not add both names to allowed sets just to make tests green.
Do not ignore unknown capabilities.

tasks.title: Enforce allowed_task_kinds in HermesAdapter runtime
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter checks blocked_task_kinds but does not clearly enforce allowed_task_kinds. A mode not blocked may pass until later capability checks or unexpected behavior.
implementation_notes: Check allowed_task_kinds before mode-specific capability checks.
If allowed_task_kinds is empty, decide explicitly whether that means no tasks or default list. Prefer no tasks for fail-closed behavior unless config model already fills defaults.
acceptance_criteria: HermesAdapter denies any mode not in config.allowed_task_kinds with reason_code='task_kind_not_allowed'.
Blocked task kind still returns task_kind_blocked or stricter denial.
Custom config allowed_task_kinds=['plan_only'] causes review, summarize, patch_propose and research_limited to be denied.
Default config still allows phase-1 task kinds intended by profile.
Denial occurs before remote Hermes HTTP call.
test_requirements: Add test with mocked client that would fail if called; verify disallowed mode does not call client.
Add test for custom allowed_task_kinds.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on blocked_task_kinds.
Do not let unknown modes reach prompt construction.

tasks.title: Separate task_kind from adapter mode explicitly
tasks.problem: The adapter currently treats mode strings like plan_only/review/patch_propose as both execution mode and task kind. This is okay for phase 1 but can become confusing when routed from higher-level task kinds like analysis or coding.
implementation_notes: Introduce explicit adapter_mode values if needed: plan_only, review, summarize, patch_propose, research_limited.
Keep task_kind in trace separately from adapter_mode.
acceptance_criteria: Trace includes adapter_mode and task_kind/requested_task_kind separately when available.
ToolRouter can select Hermes for task_kind='analysis' while adapter runs mode='review'.
ToolRouter can select Hermes for task_kind='patch_propose' while adapter runs mode='patch_propose'.
No route maps coding directly to patch_propose unless explicitly requested by policy.
Docs or code comments explain the distinction.
test_requirements: Add routing test for analysis -> Hermes review mode path if integration exists.
Add trace assertion for adapter_mode.
anti_patterns: Do not use arbitrary user prompt text to choose adapter_mode.
Do not auto-upgrade coding to patch_propose without policy.

tasks.title: Replace endpoint classification with urllib.parse + ipaddress
tasks.problem: _classify_endpoint uses simple string checks and misses IPv6 localhost, host.docker.internal, private HTTPS URLs, most 172.16/12 ranges, and hostname resolution edge cases.
implementation_notes: Move endpoint classifier into a small helper module if it grows.
Use urllib.parse.urlparse to extract hostname.
Use ipaddress.ip_address for literal IPs.
Treat localhost, 127.0.0.0/8, ::1 as local.
Treat RFC1918 private IP ranges as private_network.
Treat host.docker.internal as private_network or local_bridge, not cloud.
Do not perform DNS resolution in the classifier unless explicitly designed with timeout and SSRF considerations.
acceptance_criteria: http://localhost:8000 classifies as local.
http://127.0.0.1:8000 classifies as local.
http://[::1]:8000 classifies as local.
http://host.docker.internal:8000 classifies as private_network or local_bridge.
https://10.0.0.5:8000 classifies as private_network.
https://172.16.0.1 through https://172.31.255.255 classify as private_network.
https://192.168.1.20 classifies as private_network.
https://ollama.com or https://api.example.com classify as cloud.
Malformed/empty URLs classify as unknown or cloud-safe-deny, not local.
test_requirements: Add endpoint classification parameterized tests for all listed cases.
Add test proving private https is not classified as cloud.
anti_patterns: Do not use substring checks like 'localhost' in full URL only.
Do not classify unknown hostnames as local.

tasks.title: Compute effective_cloud_allowed once and use everywhere
tasks.problem: cloud_allowed exists in both HermesAdapterConfig and ExecutionEnvelope.model_policy. The current logic is safe but split across several checks, making it easy to drift.
implementation_notes: Add helper _effective_cloud_allowed(envelope) -> bool.
Use it for endpoint cloud blocking and context sensitive allowance.
Trace both config_cloud_allowed, envelope_cloud_allowed and effective_cloud_allowed.
acceptance_criteria: effective_cloud_allowed is true only when config.cloud_allowed and envelope.model_policy.cloud_allowed are both true.
Cloud endpoint with either flag false is denied with cloud_blocked before prompt construction.
Sensitive context is allowed to reach cloud only when effective_cloud_allowed is true and sensitivity policy permits it.
Trace includes effective_cloud_allowed and endpoint_classification.
Existing cloud-block tests still pass.
test_requirements: Parameterized tests for four combinations of config.cloud_allowed and envelope.model_policy.cloud_allowed.
Test trace fields for effective_cloud_allowed.
anti_patterns: Do not use config.cloud_allowed alone.
Do not use envelope.model_policy.cloud_allowed alone.

tasks.title: Harden sensitive context checks beyond enum string suffix
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter checks sensitive cloud context via str(getattr(...)).lower().endswith(('secret','confidential')). This is brittle and duplicates ContextConverter logic.
implementation_notes: Use ContextSensitivity enum directly where possible.
Centralize sensitivity decision in helper function.
Consider adding restricted/internal sensitivity handling if such enum values exist later.
acceptance_criteria: Cloud endpoint denies blocks with ContextSensitivity.secret and ContextSensitivity.confidential before prompt construction unless explicit policy permits.
Decision does not rely on string suffix matching.
ContextConverter and HermesAdapter use the same sensitivity helper or equivalent shared logic.
Trace records which block origin_ids caused sensitivity_blocked without leaking content.
Tests cover enum values and string fallback values if backward compatibility is needed.
test_requirements: Add tests for secret and confidential cloud block.
Add test ensuring public context can go to cloud only when cloud is otherwise allowed.
anti_patterns: Do not inspect secret content to decide whether it is secret.
Do not leak sensitive block content in denial reason.

tasks.title: Improve context truncation accounting and provenance
tasks.problem: ContextConverter truncates entry strings directly. This can cut provenance labels, cut UTF-8 semantic text awkwardly, and does not include a visible truncation marker in prompt_text.
implementation_notes: Keep provenance prefix intact where possible.
Truncate content portion after source label.
Append explicit marker like [truncated_for_budget].
Record original length and included length in truncated metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Truncated prompt still includes complete source provenance label.
Truncated prompt includes an explicit truncation marker.
ContextConversionResult.truncated includes origin_id, reason_code, original_chars and included_chars.
total_chars does not exceed max_context_chars.
No secret or suspicious skipped content appears in prompt_text.
test_requirements: Add truncation test with very small max_context_chars.
Add assertion that source label survives truncation.
anti_patterns: Do not silently cut context without marker.
Do not drop provenance to save chars unless impossible and recorded.

tasks.title: Expand prompt injection scanner patterns safely
tasks.problem: ContextConverter has a useful but small prompt-injection pattern list. It should cover the same threat style documented for Hermes context files and Ananta policy: instruction override, hidden comments, credential exfiltration, .env access, tool override, invisible unicode, bidi controls.
implementation_notes: Add conservative regexes with clear reason codes.
Keep false positives manageable; blocked content should be traceable.
Do not implement complex ML-based detection here.
acceptance_criteria: Scanner detects 'ignore previous instructions'.
Scanner detects hidden HTML comments with suspicious instruction text.
Scanner detects zero-width and bidi override characters.
Scanner detects attempts to cat .env or credentials.
Scanner detects exfiltration commands using curl/wget with token/API key variables.
Suspicious blocks are skipped or wrapped according to existing policy; default is skip for Hermes cloud/worker prompt.
test_requirements: Parameterized tests for each new pattern.
False-positive sanity test for harmless command documentation if desired.
anti_patterns: Do not let suspicious content pass because it is in repository docs.
Do not expose suspicious content raw in trace.

tasks.title: Add health fallback from /health to /v1/models
tasks.problem: HermesHttpClient.health only calls {base_url}/health. OpenAI-compatible servers often expose /v1/models but not /health, so a working Hermes/OpenAI-compatible endpoint may be marked unavailable.
implementation_notes: Try /health first for native Hermes if available.
If /health returns 404, try /v1/models.
Do not perform a model generation as default health check.
Return which probe succeeded in health payload.
acceptance_criteria: If /health succeeds, health status is ready and probe='health'.
If /health returns 404 and /v1/models succeeds, health status is ready and probe='models'.
If both fail, status maps to unauthorized/unavailable/degraded according to error.
Health check remains cheap and bounded by timeout.
Tests do not require real network.
test_requirements: Mock test for /health success.
Mock test for /health 404 then /v1/models success.
Mock test for both failing.
anti_patterns: Do not send a chat completion as normal health check.
Do not mark 404 /health as unavailable before trying /v1/models.

tasks.title: Fix base_url path joining for /v1 endpoints
tasks.problem: HermesHttpClient always calls f'{base_url}/v1/chat/completions'. If config base_url already includes /v1, the effective URL becomes /v1/v1/chat/completions.
implementation_notes: Normalize base URL into API root and endpoint path helper.
Support both base_url='http://host:8000' and base_url='http://host:8000/v1'.
Same for /v1/models.
acceptance_criteria: base_url='http://localhost:8000' produces chat URL http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions.
base_url='http://localhost:8000/v1' produces chat URL http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions, not /v1/v1.
base_url with trailing slash is normalized correctly.
Health fallback /v1/models uses same path helper.
Existing chat_success test is updated to include /v1 base_url variant.
test_requirements: Add URL-building unit tests.
Add client chat test with base_url ending in /v1.
anti_patterns: Do not require users to know whether to include /v1 without clear support.
Do not string-concatenate endpoints in multiple places.

tasks.title: Add JSON decode error mapping in HermesHttpClient
tasks.problem: _post_json and _get_json call json.loads(raw) without mapping JSONDecodeError. A malformed upstream response can escape as generic exception instead of structured HermesClientError.
implementation_notes: Catch json.JSONDecodeError around response parsing.
Return HermesClientError(code='hermes_invalid_json_response').
Do not include raw response body beyond a small sanitized snippet.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed JSON response from POST maps to HermesClientError code hermes_invalid_json_response.
Malformed JSON response from GET maps to HermesClientError code hermes_invalid_json_response.
Adapter turns this into failed/degraded WorkerResult with reason code preserved.
No raw huge/malicious response is logged or exposed.
test_requirements: Mock urlopen returning invalid JSON for POST and GET.
Adapter test for invalid_json_response path.
anti_patterns: Do not let upstream malformed JSON crash the worker route.

tasks.title: Add max_tokens and temperature policy passthrough
tasks.problem: HermesHttpClient supports max_tokens and temperature, but HermesAdapter does not appear to pass model_policy.max_tokens or configured temperature. This can make calls less bounded.
implementation_notes: Add optional config default_temperature and max_output_tokens if needed.
Use envelope.model_policy.max_tokens when set, bounded by config max if present.
Keep defaults conservative.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter passes max_tokens to client when envelope.model_policy.max_tokens is set.
Configured maximum prevents excessive max_tokens if implemented.
Temperature is deterministic/low by default for structured JSON tasks, e.g. 0.0-0.3.
Trace records effective max_tokens and temperature without bloating logs.
Existing mocks are updated to accept these kwargs.
test_requirements: Test that model_policy.max_tokens reaches client.
Test configured cap if added.
anti_patterns: Do not allow unbounded output for structured adapter calls.

tasks.title: Replace post-construction ArtifactRef.kind mutation
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter constructs ArtifactRef with a default kind and then mutates artifact.kind for patch/summarize/research. This is avoidable and weakens clarity/immutability assumptions.
implementation_notes: Create _artifact_kind_for_mode(mode) helper.
Determine kind before ArtifactRef construction.
Remove type-ignore mutation comments.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact kind is computed before ArtifactRef construction.
No assignment to artifact.kind after construction remains in HermesAdapter.
plan_only -> plan_artifact.
review -> review_artifact or verification_artifact by explicit rule.
summarize -> summary_artifact.
patch_propose -> patch_artifact.
research_limited -> research_artifact.
Existing artifact mapping tests pass.
test_requirements: Update artifact mapping test to check all modes.
anti_patterns: Do not mutate Pydantic models after creation unless intentional and documented.

tasks.title: Use accurate audit event types for parse and unsafe output
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter emits policy_denied for parse errors. Parse errors and unsafe side-effect claims are output validation failures, not necessarily policy denials.
implementation_notes: Introduce event types such as adapter_parse_error, adapter_unsafe_output, remote_output_invalid.
Keep policy_denied only for actual preflight/policy denial.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed JSON emits adapter_parse_error or remote_output_invalid, not policy_denied.
Unsafe side-effect claim emits adapter_unsafe_output.
Actual cloud_blocked/task_kind_blocked/missing_capability still emit policy/preflight denial where appropriate.
Audit tests assert exact event types.
No secrets/raw full response appear in audit payload.
test_requirements: Add audit tests for parse_error and unsafe_output.
Update existing audit success/denial tests.
anti_patterns: Do not use one generic event type for all failures.

tasks.title: Add degraded_state details to degraded WorkerResults
tasks.problem: _degraded currently returns status degraded and warnings, but does not populate WorkerResult.degraded_state. This loses machine-readable degraded details.
implementation_notes: Use existing DegradedState model.
Include reason and capabilities_unavailable when applicable.
acceptance_criteria: WorkerResult.degraded_state is set for disabled_config, context_missing_or_sensitive, research_context_missing and similar degraded cases.
degraded_state.reason equals the reason_code.
Warnings remain human-readable but machine-readable state is present.
Tests assert degraded_state for at least disabled_config and context_missing_or_sensitive.
test_requirements: Add degraded_state assertions.
anti_patterns: Do not encode all degraded detail only in summary string.

tasks.title: Add prompt/context/response hash consistency tests
tasks.problem: Trace and artifacts include hashes, but there should be tests proving they are stable and correspond to the same context/payload across artifact metadata and TraceBundle.
implementation_notes: Use deterministic mock payload and context.
Avoid testing exact hash values if helper may change; test equality across fields where intended.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact metadata context_hash equals hash of converted prompt_text for the same execution.
Trace event hermes_prompt_built context_hash equals artifact metadata context_hash or documented equivalent.
Trace event hermes_result_parsed response_hash is present for success.
Changing context changes context_hash.
Changing payload changes content_hash.
test_requirements: Add deterministic hash consistency tests.
anti_patterns: Do not log raw prompt to make debugging easier by default.

tasks.title: Add real TaskScopedExecutionService Hermes execution path
tasks.problem: The adapter and router exist, but the real task execution service must clearly call HermesAdapter when ToolRouter selects Hermes. Otherwise Hermes remains a tested side path, not an integrated worker.
implementation_notes: Find the propose path first; do not start with execute mutation path.
Add a small adapter invocation helper rather than cluttering TaskScopedExecutionService.
Support plan_only first, then review and patch_propose.
acceptance_criteria: When ToolRouter selected_target='hermes' for a proposal-safe task, TaskScopedExecutionService invokes HermesAdapter, not SGPT/OpenCode/native fallback.
TaskScopedExecutionService builds or receives an ExecutionEnvelope for the Hermes call.
TaskScopedExecutionService resolves bounded context blocks through existing context manager/research bridge where practical.
Hermes WorkerResult artifacts are persisted or returned in the same structure expected by existing task proposal flow.
If Hermes returns denied/degraded/failed, TaskScopedExecutionService records that and either returns it or uses a policy-approved fallback with trace.
No patch_apply or command_execute path calls Hermes.
test_requirements: Mocked integration test through propose endpoint selecting Hermes.
Test that command_execute/patch_apply never calls Hermes even if requested_backend='hermes'.
Test fallback behavior when Hermes unavailable.
anti_patterns: Do not call Hermes directly from route functions.
Do not bypass ToolRouter/CapabilityRegistry for Hermes selection.
Do not call Hermes for execute_task_step mutation paths.

tasks.title: Map existing task/research context into ContextBlock list for Hermes
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter expects ContextBlock list, but TaskScopedExecutionService currently has task dicts, request data, research_context_summary and worker context metadata. A deterministic mapping is needed.
implementation_notes: Build small function build_hermes_context_blocks(task, request_data, research_context_summary, worker_context_meta).
Classify sensitivity conservatively.
Include provenance for each block.
acceptance_criteria: Task description/prompt becomes a public or internal ContextBlock with source_type='task'.
Research context prompt section becomes a ContextBlock with source_type='research_context' and proper origin_id.
Artifact refs and knowledge ids are included as metadata or bounded text, not blindly expanded.
Secrets or raw environment/config blocks are not included by default.
Mapping records skipped/omitted sources when they are too large or sensitive.
Hermes call fails degraded when required context cannot be built safely.
test_requirements: Unit tests for context mapping with task only, research context, and sensitive data.
Integration test proving context blocks reach adapter.
anti_patterns: Do not dump full task history into Hermes prompt.
Do not pass entire repository or raw artifacts by default.

tasks.title: Persist Hermes artifacts through existing artifact pipeline
tasks.problem: HermesAdapter returns ArtifactRef objects, but task flow must persist or expose them through the same artifact mechanisms used by other worker paths, otherwise results are not durable/reviewable.
implementation_notes: Use existing artifact store/sync path if available.
Do not write into workspace source tree.
Keep PatchArtifact separate from apply operation.
acceptance_criteria: PlanArtifact/ReviewArtifact/PatchArtifact returned by Hermes are visible in task response artifact_refs or equivalent existing field.
PatchArtifact content is stored as artifact data or structured payload, not applied to workspace.
Artifact metadata includes source='hermes', model, context_hash, content_hash and requires_approval_for_apply for patches.
Task history records Hermes artifact creation.
Artifact persistence failure results in failed/degraded task result, not silent success.
test_requirements: Mocked task-flow test verifying artifact refs in response.
Test artifact persistence failure path.
anti_patterns: Do not treat WorkerResult.artifacts only as in-memory temporary data.
Do not git apply Hermes patch output.

tasks.title: Add Hermes fallback policy in task flow
tasks.problem: If Hermes is selected but unavailable/degraded, the system needs explicit fallback behavior. Silent fallback hides routing and security decisions.
implementation_notes: Fallback should be allowed only if ToolRouter decision includes eligible alternatives or policy permits fallback.
Trace should record fallback_from_hermes.
acceptance_criteria: If Hermes unavailable and an eligible alternative exists, fallback occurs only when policy allows it.
Fallback result includes trace/policy observation that Hermes failed/unavailable and which backend was used instead.
If no eligible fallback exists, task result is degraded/no_backend_available, not arbitrary SGPT/OpenCode fallback.
Fallback never increases capability risk beyond original grant.
Tests cover Hermes unavailable with fallback and without fallback.
test_requirements: Add fallback integration tests.
anti_patterns: Do not silently fall back to a more powerful backend.
Do not hide Hermes failure from task response.

tasks.title: Ensure Hermes path cannot enter execute_task_step mutation branch
tasks.problem: Hermes must stay proposal/review-only in phase 1. The execute_task_step path includes command execution, patch/native runtime and workspace snapshot logic; Hermes must not be callable there for mutation modes.
implementation_notes: Add explicit guard near execute_task_step routing/input handling.
If requested_backend='hermes' for execute mutation, return denied or route only to safe verification mode if explicitly supported.
acceptance_criteria: execute_task_step with requested_backend='hermes' and command_execute-like request returns denied before any Hermes HTTP call.
execute_task_step with patch_apply-like request returns denied before any Hermes HTTP call.
No workspace snapshot/apply logic is reached through Hermes branch for mutation task kinds.
Audit/trace records reason hermes_phase1_no_execute_mutation.
Existing native/OpenCode execute behavior is not broken.
test_requirements: Add endpoint/integration tests for execute_task_step blocked with Hermes.
Mock client assertion: Hermes client not called.
anti_patterns: Do not implement Hermes execute as a shortcut to get patch_apply working.

tasks.title: Split giant Hermes test file into focused test modules
tasks.problem: tests/test_hermes_worker_adapter_track.py is broad and becoming too large. Smaller test files make it easier for small models and humans to maintain.
implementation_notes: Do this after key fixes if refactor noise would interfere.
Keep shared fixtures in tests helpers if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Tests are split into focused files such as test_hermes_config.py, test_hermes_http_client.py, test_hermes_context.py, test_hermes_parser.py, test_hermes_adapter_modes.py, test_hermes_routing.py, test_hermes_task_flow.py.
No test coverage is removed.
Common fake clients/fixtures are reused without copy-paste explosion.
pytest still discovers all tests.
File names make failure source obvious.
test_requirements: Run full pytest subset for Hermes tests.
anti_patterns: Do not mix task-flow integration tests with parser unit tests in one giant file forever.

tasks.title: Add explicit no-network/no-file side effect regression tests
tasks.problem: Hermes phase 1 must not create file, shell, browser, MCP or direct network side effects except its configured API call. Tests should prove this at adapter boundary.
implementation_notes: Use monkeypatch to fail if subprocess, open write, pathlib write_text, or unintended network helpers are called.
Allow only HermesHttpClient mock call.
acceptance_criteria: plan_only does not call subprocess or write files.
review does not call subprocess or write files.
patch_propose does not call subprocess, write files, or apply patches.
research_limited does not perform arbitrary network calls; it only uses provided context plus Hermes API call.
Any Hermes output claiming side effects is rejected.
test_requirements: Add side-effect guard tests with monkeypatch.
anti_patterns: Do not assume no side effects just because code currently looks simple.

tasks.title: Add strict output schema tests per mode
tasks.problem: Parser has common schema and some mode checks, but mode-specific coverage should be stronger, especially for patch_propose and research_limited.
implementation_notes: Keep schema simple but explicit.
Avoid overfitting to one sample JSON.
acceptance_criteria: plan_only valid payload passes.
review with non-list findings fails.
patch_propose without patch_unified_diff and patch_description fails.
patch_propose without touched_files fails.
research_limited without claims fails.
no_side_effects_claimed=false fails for every mode.
artifact_type incompatible with mode either fails or is normalized by documented rule.
test_requirements: Add parser tests for every acceptance criterion.
anti_patterns: Do not accept mode-incompatible artifact_type silently unless documented.

tasks.title: Add cloud-sensitive context matrix tests
tasks.problem: Cloud blocking and sensitive context are central to Ananta's security model and need matrix coverage, not only one or two spot checks.
implementation_notes: Use parameterized tests over endpoint_classification, config.cloud_allowed, envelope.cloud_allowed, sensitivity.
acceptance_criteria: Cloud endpoint + public context + both cloud flags true succeeds or reaches mocked client.
Cloud endpoint + either cloud flag false is denied before prompt construction.
Cloud endpoint + secret/confidential context is denied or excluded according to explicit policy; default must not leak.
Local/private endpoint + secret context is still filtered unless allow_sensitive policy says otherwise.
Trace records denial reason and endpoint classification.
test_requirements: Add parameterized matrix tests.
anti_patterns: Do not test only localhost and assume cloud path is safe.

tasks.title: Run and record Hermes pytest subset in track status
tasks.problem: The fixup should not be considered done unless the Hermes-specific tests are runnable and documented.
implementation_notes: Add command examples to docs or comments.
Do not require live Hermes service for default test command.
acceptance_criteria: Documented test command exists, e.g. pytest tests/test_hermes_*.py.
Default command does not need API keys or live Hermes endpoint.
Optional live test command is separate and gated by env var.
All fixup tests pass locally/CI before marking done.
Track summary can be updated with pass/fail state after execution.
test_requirements: Run Hermes unit + mocked integration tests.
anti_patterns: Do not make CI depend on Ollama Cloud/Hermes account.

tasks.title: Update Hermes worker docs with actual current behavior
tasks.problem: Docs/todos describe intended behavior, but the actual implementation now needs current operator/developer docs: supported modes, config, health, routing, limitations and known safety boundaries.
implementation_notes: Do not overpromise production readiness.
Mention phase-1 limitations clearly.
acceptance_criteria: Docs list actual supported modes: plan_only, review, summarize, patch_propose, research_limited if implemented.
Docs state Hermes cannot patch_apply or command_execute.
Docs explain config.cloud_allowed and envelope.model_policy.cloud_allowed interaction.
Docs show both base_url with and without /v1 if supported.
Docs explain health fallback /health -> /v1/models if implemented.
Docs include recommended safe first model and local/cloud warning.
test_requirements: Docs reviewed against code/config names.
anti_patterns: Do not document planned features as already working.

tasks.title: Improve diagnostics to include eligibility and routing reasons
tasks.problem: Hermes diagnostics show health/config state, but operators also need to know why Hermes was or was not eligible for a task.
implementation_notes: Keep diagnostics secret-free.
Reuse ToolRouter alternatives when possible.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics include allowed_task_kinds, blocked_task_kinds, allowed capabilities, denied capabilities, endpoint classification and health state.
Routing decision alternatives include disabled_by_feature_flag, adapter_disabled, missing_capabilities, governance_risk_blocked, backend_unavailable as applicable.
Diagnostics do not expose API key values or raw prompts.
Tests assert diagnostics redaction.
test_requirements: Diagnostics tests for ready, disabled, cloud_blocked and missing_capability cases.
anti_patterns: Do not make users guess why Hermes was not selected.

tasks.title: Add rollout gate before enabling Hermes by default
tasks.problem: BridgeAdapterRegistry default feature flag is false, but HermesAdapterConfig feature_flag_enabled defaults true. Rollout behavior should be explicit and consistent to avoid accidental enablement.
implementation_notes: Decide a single source of truth for feature flag. Prefer Hub/agent config controls registry and adapter construction.
If adapter config has a local feature flag, document how it combines with global flag.
acceptance_criteria: Default application config does not route to Hermes unless explicitly enabled.
BridgeAdapterRegistry and HermesAdapter agree on disabled_by_feature_flag behavior.
If global flag disabled, HermesAdapter is not called or immediately denies without network call.
Tests cover global flag disabled, adapter enabled; and global flag enabled, adapter disabled.
Docs state exact flags required to enable Hermes.
test_requirements: Feature flag integration tests.
anti_patterns: Do not have two flags with contradictory defaults and no combination rule.

tasks.title: Update fixup track after implementation with done status and evidence
tasks.problem: This file should become a useful implementation tracker, not just a static review artifact.
implementation_notes: As tasks are completed, update status and optionally add evidence fields or commit refs.
Keep JSON valid.
acceptance_criteria: Completed tasks are marked done with commit/evidence note if schema allows extension.
Blocked tasks include blocker reason.
Summary counts are updated after meaningful batches.
Critical path status reflects actual implementation state.
JSON remains parseable.
test_requirements: json.loads todo.hermes-fixup.json succeeds.
anti_patterns: Do not leave status summary stale after large fixup batch.

definition_of_done_global: plan_only is validated as plan_only, not summarize.
Capability vocabulary is consistent across Hermes profile, CapabilityRegistry, ToolRouter and HermesAdapter.
Endpoint classification is robust for localhost, IPv6 localhost, Docker host, RFC1918 private networks and cloud hosts.
Cloud/sensitive context policy uses a single effective_cloud_allowed decision and shared sensitivity logic.
Health works with /health and OpenAI-compatible /v1/models fallback.
base_url works with and without trailing /v1.
Parse errors and unsafe outputs use accurate audit event types.
Hermes is invoked through the real TaskScopedExecutionService path only for safe proposal/review modes.
Hermes is never invoked for patch_apply or command_execute in phase 1.
Hermes artifacts are persisted/exposed through the existing task artifact flow.
Security regression tests prove no direct file/shell/tool side effects and no cloud-sensitive leakage.
Docs accurately describe current implemented behavior and rollout flags.
summary_notes: This fixup track is based on real code review of the current Hermes adapter implementation.
The most urgent issues are capability naming consistency, plan_only validation, endpoint/cloud policy, health/base_url robustness, and real TaskScopedExecutionService integration.
Do not mark Hermes production-ready until the minimum_safe_fix_slice and security regression tests are complete.
